themes.instructions: For each clinical scenario below, select the single most appropriate management from the option list. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.
options: No treatment; repeat thyroid function tests in 4 weeks
Urgent endocrinology referral for thyroid storm management

stems.stem: A 30-year-old woman with Graves' disease, well controlled on carbimazole, confirms a pregnancy at 6 weeks' gestation.
stems.explanation: Carbimazole in the first trimester is associated with aplasia cutis and embryopathy. Women with Graves' disease should be switched to propylthiouracil for the first trimester; many units then switch back to carbimazole after organogenesis because of the hepatotoxicity risk of prolonged propylthiouracil.

stems.stem: A 34-year-old woman on 100 micrograms of levothyroxine for autoimmune hypothyroidism attends the booking clinic at 8 weeks' gestation, clinically euthyroid.
stems.explanation: Thyroxine requirements rise by roughly 30–50% in early pregnancy. Standard practice is to increase levothyroxine (commonly by 25–50 micrograms) as soon as pregnancy is confirmed and monitor TSH each trimester, keeping TSH in the trimester-specific range.

stems.stem: A 27-year-old woman at 10 weeks' gestation with hyperemesis gravidarum has a suppressed TSH, mildly raised free T4, negative TSH-receptor antibodies and no goitre or eye signs.
stems.explanation: This is gestational transient thyrotoxicosis driven by hCG, typical with hyperemesis. It settles as hCG falls after the first trimester, antithyroid drugs are not indicated — observe and repeat thyroid function tests.

themes.instructions: For each woman described, select the single most likely diagnosis from the option list. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

stems.stem: A 38-year-old woman, 3 weeks postnatal after a twin pregnancy, presents with progressive breathlessness, orthopnoea and a displaced apex beat. Echocardiography shows a dilated left ventricle with an ejection fraction of 32%.
stems.explanation: New heart failure with left ventricular systolic dysfunction towards the end of pregnancy or in the months after delivery, without another identifiable cause, defines peripartum cardiomyopathy. Multiple pregnancy, hypertension and increasing maternal age are recognised risk factors.

stems.stem: A 29-year-old recent migrant at 24 weeks' gestation presents with breathlessness on exertion and haemoptysis. Auscultation reveals a loud first heart sound and a mid-diastolic rumbling murmur at the apex.
stems.explanation: Rheumatic mitral stenosis classically decompensates in mid-pregnancy as plasma volume and heart rate rise. The loud S1 with an apical mid-diastolic murmur is characteristic; it is the most common rheumatic valve lesion encountered in pregnancy worldwide.

stems.stem: A 32-year-old woman with Marfan syndrome at 34 weeks' gestation presents with sudden severe tearing chest pain radiating to the back. Blood pressure differs by 25 mmHg between arms.
stems.explanation: Sudden tearing interscapular pain with an inter-arm blood pressure differential in a woman with Marfan syndrome strongly suggests aortic dissection, for which pregnancy — particularly the third trimester — is a recognised precipitant.
